violations.description: General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. Observed food employee sweep the floor and handle the dust pan and he did not wash his hands afterward. Food employee also touched his hair without washing his hands. Hands should be washed after touching personal items and handling cleaning items.

Refrigerated Storage. — --Refrigerator or cold storage was insufficient or ineffective at maintaining potentially hazardous food at a temperature of 41F or less. Food items in the refrigerated table under the oven were found at 46-54.8F This table also has ice built up inside the right side of the unit. Refrigerated units should be be able to maintain potentially hazardous food items at 41F or below. **Potentially hazardoud food items should be discarded.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned as frequently as required. Shelving units in the walk-in cooler were found dirty during inspection. These should be cleaned and maintained clean.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Exposed studs, joists or rafters were not covered with smooth, nonabsorbent and easily cleanable material. Storage closet off of the dining area has exposed studs along 1 wall. Studs should be covered with smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable material. Ceiling tiles in this storage closet are acoustic tiles. Ceiling should be covered with smooth, nonabsorbent, and easily cleanable material.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Clean, employee dressing area, cleaning equipment storage, living/sleeping quarters separate (1 pt deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
